applyTo: "**"
---

# BBE-Charts — GitHub Copilot Instructions

## What This Project Is

bbe-charts is the Helm chart repository for the **Brains Beyond Expectations (BBE)** home-lab platform. It provides a media server stack and network infrastructure stack as Helm umbrella charts, consumed by the [bbe-quest CLI](https://github.com/Brains-Beyond-Expectations/bbe-quest) at runtime.

Charts are hosted at `https://brains-beyond-expectations.github.io/bbe-charts`.

---

## Repository Structure

```
charts/
├── autobrr/       ← download automation
├── bazarr/        ← subtitle manager
├── blocky/        ← DNS proxy + ad-blocker
├── prowlarr/      ← indexer manager (*arr proxy)
├── radarr/        ← movie PVR
├── sonarr/        ← TV show PVR
├── bbe-media/     ← umbrella: bazarr + jellyfin + jellyseerr + prowlarr + radarr + sonarr
└── bbe-networking/ ← umbrella: blocky + ingress-nginx + metallb + MetalLB CRs

library.yaml          ← AUTOGENERATED — never edit by hand
required-versions.yaml ← source of truth for chart/CLI version requirements
lib-gen/              ← Go tool that generates library.yaml
```

---

## Critical Rules

### `library.yaml` is always autogenerated

`library.yaml` starts with `# AUTOGENERATED FILE, DO NOT EDIT!!!`. Never modify it directly. Always:
1. Change chart versions in `charts/<name>/Chart.yaml`
2. Change CLI version requirements in `required-versions.yaml`
3. Run `cd lib-gen && go run main.go` to regenerate

### No third-party dependencies in lib-gen without discussion

lib-gen uses only `blang/semver`, `spf13/viper`, and `stretchr/testify`. Reach for stdlib first.

---

## Standard Chart Values Shape

Every individual chart (`sonarr`, `radarr`, `bazarr`, `prowlarr`, `blocky`, `autobrr`) exposes the same top-level keys:

```yaml
image:
  repository: <image>
  tag: ""                 # defaults to Chart appVersion
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

replicas: 1
service:
  type: ClusterIP         # LoadBalancer for blocky
  port: <app-port>

ingress:
  enabled: false
  ingressClassName: ""
  hosts: []
  tls: []

config:                   # config PVC (all charts)
  enabled: true
  size: 1Gi
  accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
  existingClaim: ""
  retain: false

media:                    # media PVC (bazarr, radarr, sonarr only)
  enabled: true
  size: 50Gi
  accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
  existingClaim: ""
  retain: false

resources: {}
env: []
```

Maintain this consistent shape when adding new individual charts.

---

## Testing

Charts use [helm-unittest](https://github.com/helm-unittest/helm-unittest). Tests live in `charts/<name>/tests/`.

```bash
# Test a single chart
helm unittest charts/sonarr

# Test all charts
helm unittest charts/*
```

lib-gen tests use `stretchr/testify`:

```bash
cd lib-gen && make test
```

---

## How bbe-quest Uses This Repo

At runtime, the bbe-quest CLI:
1. Downloads `library.yaml` via HTTP (GitHub raw URL)
2. Picks the most recent `LibraryEntry` where `min-bbe-cli ≤ running CLI version`
3. Calls `helm repo add + helm install/upgrade` for each chart in that entry

Only `bbe-media` and `bbe-networking` appear in `library.yaml`. Individual charts are sub-dependencies.

---

## library.yaml Format

```yaml
library:
  - min-bbe-cli: "0.6.1"
    list-revision: 2        # increments every time a chart version changes
    charts:
      - name: bbe-media
        version: 0.1.1
        repositoryUrl: https://brains-beyond-expectations.github.io/bbe-charts
        repositoryName: bbe
      - name: bbe-networking
        version: 0.1.0
        repositoryUrl: https://brains-beyond-expectations.github.io/bbe-charts
        repositoryName: bbe
```

Entries are sorted descending by `min-bbe-cli`, then descending by `list-revision`. The full history is preserved.

---

## Umbrella Charts

### bbe-media
Bundles: `bazarr`, `jellyfin` (external), `jellyseerr` (external OCI), `prowlarr`, `radarr`, `sonarr`.
`values.yaml` is empty — all configuration goes through sub-chart prefixes (`radarr.image.tag`, etc.).

### bbe-networking
Bundles: `blocky`, `ingress-nginx` (external), `metallb` (external).
Also creates MetalLB `IPAddressPool` + `L2Advertisement` resources via templates.  
Required values: `bbe.metallb.blocky.ipAddressPool` and `ingress-nginx.controller.service.loadBalancerIP`.

---

## Adding or Updating Charts

**Bump a chart version:**
1. Edit `version` (+ `appVersion` if app changed) in `charts/<name>/Chart.yaml`
2. Run `cd lib-gen && go run main.go`
3. Update umbrella dependency version + run `helm dependency update charts/<umbrella>` if applicable

**Add a new umbrella chart:**
1. Create `charts/<name>/` with standard structure
2. Add entry to `required-versions.yaml`
3. Run `cd lib-gen && go run main.go`
4. Add docs at `docs/charts/<name>.md`

Documentation lives in `docs/`. Each chart has a corresponding `docs/charts/<name>.md`.
